basics.name: Donald Thomas Stolz
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer / Head of Engineering
basics.summary: Senior engineer with over 9 years of full stack experience, specializing in driving technical development and delivering high-impact solutions within fast-paced startup teams. Proven track record of going zero-to-one on greenfield systems — from architecture through deployment. Comfortable owning the entire stack as a solo engineer or leading small teams.

work.name: Walter Grades
work.summary: Walter AI is building a ratings standard for investment newsletters. We convert newsletter recommendations into deterministic, auditable track records.
highlights: Architected and built the entire platform as sole engineer — full stack, infrastructure, and DevOps — using Next.js, NestJS, and AWS
Built AI-powered newsletter parsing pipeline using Gemini and AWS Lambda to extract stock trade recommendations from financial newsletters and generate auditable performance track records
Developed Google Chrome Extension to automate Gmail filter setup, streamlining newsletter ingestion into the platform
Currently rolling out beta; architected for scale from day one with webhook-based email ingestion via Postmark
Leveraged Claude Code to accelerate development velocity as a one-person engineering team

work.name: St. Michael's Catholic Preparatory School
work.summary: Part-time coaching role supporting the lacrosse program at St. Michael's Catholic Preparatory School in Austin, TX.
highlights: Coached 5th & 6th grade lacrosse team during Spring 2025 season, leading practice planning and player development
Designed and ran youth lacrosse practices and camps; focused on introducing new players to the sport and developing fundamental skills
Varsity assistant for Spring 2026 season, focusing on faceoff-specific skill development

work.name: Freelance
highlights: Utilized Gemini LLM to perform user personality analysis and generate engaging insights based on user interactions
Created scalable data processing features using Google Cloud Tasks
Working with GCP, Firebase, and Terraform to provide cloud solutions

work.name: soona (Acquired Trend)
work.summary: soona is your all-in-one platform to make, manage, and optimize photo + video content for all your ecommerce channels.
highlights: Spearheaded Trend technical development throughout acquisition, using TypeScript, React, and Next.js
Created and presented detailed transition documentation to onboard soona engineers, utilizing Notion, Swagger, & Miro
Led onboarding of new engineers to Trend's platform
Planned and developed auth integration feature, allowing connection between soona and Trend users
Member of soona's payment team; utilized Stripe, Postgres, Ruby on Rails, and Vue.js to enhance payment processing and invoice features
Earned AWS Solutions Architect certification
Built features to scale revenue, grew to over $150,000 monthly

work.name: Trend
work.summary: We help companies win at marketing. We connect brands with creators who provide original & specifically targeted content.
highlights: Primary engineer and technical decision-maker on revenue-driving features; grew platform to over $150,000 MRR
Led technical development initiatives while directly reporting to the CEO and CPO
Planned sprints and broke down project tickets in collaboration with the CPO using Asana
Presented product demos to team members, keeping everyone informed on ongoing builds and collecting valuable feedback for continuous improvement
Managed relationships with outside partners and contractors
Created comprehensive documentation using tools like Notion, Swagger, and Storybook
Conceptualized and defined long-term technical direction and goals, aligning the team with future growth initiatives
Built SQS-driven deadline processing system to automate creator workflow enforcement at scale
Built serverless content processing pipeline using AWS Lambda to automatically watermark and generate thumbnails for all creator image and video submissions
Built admin dashboards and tooling, improving workflows for customer success agents and sales

work.name: Asset Dash

highlights: Migrated backend and database from Heroku to Google Cloud Platform
Updated and maintained Postgres database; refactored client to Next.js, improving performance & SEO
Created new features to drive user growth and increase session duration

work.name: Unstoppable Domains
work.summary: Unstoppable Domains builds blockchain domains that improve crypto payments.
highlights: Launched innovative website builder tools, driving domain growth from approximately 100 in 01/2020 to over 15,000 by 06/2020, significantly enhancing customer acquisition and retention
Revitalized frontend with Next.js, enhancing performance & SEO
Developed analytics services using Docker, RudderStack, and Snowflake
Generated sales and UX reports, allowing the business to make informed decisions
Took lead on many large tasks such as client-side business logic refactor
Made major mobile UI & performance improvements
Setup multiple site integrations for business analytics, including Segment, Snowflake, & Mode
Implemented integrations that saved development time and cut costs, including RudderStack and Lever

work.name: Code for Fun
work.summary: Code for fun is a nonprofit organization, located in the San Francisco Bay Area, committed to exposing all students to computer science at an early age.
highlights: Led weekly programming classes of 5-8 students (ages 7-16), supporting self-paced and advanced learners across web development topics
Designed and taught full web development curriculum from scratch covering React, Node.js REST APIs, and MongoDB — culminating in a student-built TODO application
Delivered after-school programs and summer camps teaching Scratch to beginner-level students
Conducted interviews for summer camp hiring

Guided students through project-based LEGO robotics builds, developing problem-solving and fine motor skills
